name: ietoolkit
description: Stata commands designed for Impact Evaluations in particular, but also data work in general

name: stata
description: Stata Commands for Data Management and Analysis

name: iefieldkit
description: Stata commands designed for Impact Evaluations field work. These are tools that are used during/after a survey in the field for data quality monitoring.

name: dime-r-training
description: Dime Analytics R Training

name: dime-github-trainings
description: Training materials and other GitHub related information developed by DIME Analytics

name: stata-visual-library
description: Inspiration and code for data visualizatio in Stata, created and maintained by DIME Analytics.

name: r-econ-visual-library
description: This is a repository maintained by DIME Analytics and containing example graphs on how to create graphs for data analysis of Impact Evaluations using R.

name: dec-python-course
description: This repository contains all the material for the DEC Python training. This training is developed by DIME and DECID.

name: repkit
description: A Stata package with tools related to computational reproducibility

name: blackmarblepy
description: Georeferenced Rasters and Statistics of Nightlights from NASA Black Marble

name: alternative-data-for-crisis
description: Using Alternative Data to Assess Immediate Economic Impacts of Crises -- Conflict and Natural Disasters

name: wazeasy
description: Wazeasy is a Python package currently in development, designed to process Waze's data

name: ntl-training
description: Training on using nighttime lights data from NASA Black Marble for development analysis, including geospatial impact evaluations

name: MENA-FCV-economic-monitor
description: This assignment aims to compare the impacts of conflict across the Fragile, Conflict, and Violence (FCV) regions of the Middle East and North Africa (MENA), Afghanistan, and Pakistan through alternative data sources.

name: metadata-editor
description: Web tool to edit, validate, and manage metadata for Microdata (DDI Codebook), documents, tables, media, and geospatial data.

name: metadata-editor-fastapi
description: Metadata Editor FastAPI backend service for processing SPSS, Stata, and CSV files for summaries, and data dictionaries

name: metadata-editor-docs
description: Metadata Editor user and practice guide

name: GeoLink
description: GeoLink is an R package that provides easy access to various geospatial datasets, allowing seamless integration with your spatial data or household surveys.

name: macroanomaly
description: An R package for detecting anomalies in social and economic time series data

name: indonesia-traffic-analysis
description: Congestion, accessibility, and connected communities analysis for Bali using proprietary and open-source mobility data.

name: geometadatatools
description: Lightweight tools to extract, analyze, and visualize metadata from raster and vector geospatial datasets

name: data360-mcp
description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that gives LLM agents and chatbots direct access to the World Bank's Data360 Platform. Search, validate, and retrieve development indicators—from GDP and poverty to gender equality and climate—with structured metadata and time-series data, without hallucinating values.

name: MFMod-Python-Models
description: A repository for publicly released macroeconomic models to be used with the ModelFlow system

name: mega-gld
description: Automation pipeline to onboard the Global Labor Database to the Corporate Data Lake and publish its datasets to the Microdata Library

name: data-ai-chatbot
description: World Bank Data AI Chatbot: natural-language access to development data via Data360 MCP, charts and documents, and Proof-Carrying Numbers (PCN). Next.js + FastAPI. Forked from Vercel AI Chatbot. Apache-2.0 + World Bank IGO Rider.

name: space2stats-stata
description: space2stats-stata: Stata package to query satellite data processed from Space2Stats to subnational boundaries

name: pcn
description: Proof-Carrying Numbers (PCN): Trust is earned only by proof — the absence of a verification mark communicates uncertainty.

name: devCEQ
description: R package infrastructure for building fiscal microsimulation Shiny apps. Streamlines development of interactive poverty, inequality, and tax-benefit incidence analysis tools.

name: eca-resilience
description: GPS-based analysis of human mobility and urban space usage during atypical events.

name: oss-catalog
description: World Bank Open Source Code catalog external site, surfacing World Bank open source projects to make them more discoverable.

name: geoPovLearn
description: This repository hosts learning materials developed and maintained by the GeoPov team. These includes Brown Bag events (BBLs) seminars and more structured courses.

name: devPTIpack
description: R package for developing Project Targeting Index  R Shiny applications for the corporate portfolio monitoring.

name: trend-narrative
description: Python package for piecewise-linear trend detection and plain-English narrative generation for time series data.

name: open-source-kit
description: Reusable GitHub Actions workflow and Copilot skill for World Bank open source compliance.

name: FuzzyAI
description: A Python library for entity deduplication and cross‑dataset matching using fuzzy similarity for candidate generation, with optional rule‑based and LLM‑based validation and consolidation to resolve ambiguous matches

name: Geospatial-Data-Hub-Guidelines
description: Guidance for the creation of ArcGIS Hubs at the country level for the management, analysis, and collaboration of geospatial data. Guidance includes how to create a hub, request permissions, metadata framework, group guidelines, design, and how to publish.

name: MANAGE-WB
description: MANAGE-WB is a recursive dynamic Computable General Equilibrium (CGE) model implemented in GAMS, developed by the World Bank for economy-wide policy analysis across a wide range of country contexts.

name: iehfc
description: IEHFC - Shiny App for High Frequency Checks of Survey Data
